L1_headline: + SEM – John A. Saich acquired 100,000 restricted stock shares. | 07/29/25
L2_brief: John A. Saich, Executive Vice President of Select Medical Holdings, received a grant of 100,000 restricted stock shares on July 29, 2025, at no cost. The shares vest in full on July 29, 2028, representing a long-term retention award that aligns executive compensation with shareholder interests over a 3-year period.

L3_detailed.pattern_notes: This is a restricted stock grant as part of executive compensation, not an open-market purchase. The grant represents approximately 13% of the insider's total post-transaction holdings, indicating a meaningful equity stake. The 3-year cliff vesting schedule suggests the company is focused on long-term executive retention.
risks_flagged: Not a discretionary purchase - compensation grant
No 10b5-1 plan indicated as this is an award, not a sale
